name: splinter

metadata.name: splinter
metadata.summary: browser abstraction for web acceptance testing

documentation:
# splinter

Splinter is a Python framework that provides a simple and consistent interface for web application automation.

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/splinter.svg
:alt: PyPI
:target: https://pypi.org/project/splinter
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/splinter.svg
:alt: PyPI - Python Version
:target: https://github.com/cobrateam/splinter
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/github/license/cobrateam/splinter.svg
:alt: GitHub
:target: https://github.com/cobrateam/splinter/blob/master/LICENSE
```

```{image} https://github.com/cobrateam/splinter/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Build status
:target: https://github.com/cobrateam/splinter/actions/workflows/main.yml
```

- [Documentation](https://splinter.readthedocs.io)
- [Changelog](https://splinter.readthedocs.io/en/latest/news.html)

Key features:

- Easy to learn: The API is designed to be intuitive and quick to pick up.
- Faster to code: Automate browser interactions quickly and reliably without fighting the tool.
- Powerful: Designed for real world use cases, it guards against common automation quirks.
- Flexible: Access to lower level tools is never hidden. Break out into raw Selenium at any time.
- Robust: Support is available for multiple automation drivers (Selenium, Django, Flask, ZopeTestBrowser).

## Example

```python
from splinter import Browser


browser = Browser('firefox')
browser.visit('http://google.com')
browser.find_by_name('q').fill('splinter - python acceptance testing for web applications')
browser.find_by_name('btnK').click()

if browser.is_text_present('splinter.readthedocs.io'):
    print("Yes, the official website was found!")
else:
    print("No, it wasn't found... We need to improve our SEO techniques")

browser.quit()
```

### Getting Started

- [Installation](https://splinter.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install/install.html)
- [Tutorial](https://splinter.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tutorial.html)

### Pytest Plugins

- [pytest-splinter](http://pytest-splinter.readthedocs.io), Splinter plugin for the [py.test](http://docs.pytest.org) runner.

### Page Objects

Support for page objects is available through the following package:

- [Stere](https://stere.readthedocs.io/)

functions.name: splinter.retry._retry
documentation:
Retry a function that should return a truthy value until a timeout is hit.

Arguments:
    fn: Function to retry
    timeout: Number of seconds to retry.

Returns:
    bool - True if the function returns a truthy value before the timeout, else False.

classes.name: splinter.element_list.ElementList

methods.name: splinter.element_list.ElementList.first
documentation:
An alias to the first element of the list.

Example:

    >>> assert element_list[0] == element_list.first

methods.name: splinter.element_list.ElementList.last
documentation:
An alias to the last element of the list.

Example:

    >>> assert element_list[-1] == element_list.last

methods.name: splinter.element_list.ElementList.is_empty
documentation:
Check if the ElementList is empty.

Returns:
    bool: True if the list is empty, else False

methods.name: splinter.element_list.ElementList.__len__
documentation: __len__ checks the internal container.

methods.name: splinter.element_list.ElementList.__repr__
documentation: Return the repr of the internal container.

Collection of elements.

Each member of the collection is by default an instance
of {class}`ElementAPI <splinter.driver.ElementAPI>`.

Beyond the traditional list methods, `ElementList` provides some
other methods, listed below.

There is a peculiar behavior on ElementList: you never get an
`IndexError`. Instead, you get an {class}`ElementDoesNotExist
<splinter.exceptions.ElementDoesNotExist>` exception when trying to
access a non-existent item:

> ```pycon
> >>> element_list = ElementList([])
> >>> element_list[0] # raises ElementDoesNotExist
> ```

functions.name: splinter.driver.xpath_utils._concat_xpath_from_str

Take a string and splice it into an xpath concat locator.

Arguments:

functions.name: splinter.driver.xpath_utils._recurse
documentation:
Take a string, split it, then build a new string from the parts.

The position of the character used to split the original string will be
replaced in the new string by cap.

Arguments:
    text (str): Text block to scan.
    split_on (str): A character to remove via splitting.
    wrapper (str): The wrapper for each sub-block.
    replacer (str): The replacement for the removed character.
    inner (bool): True if already inside a sub-block.

Returns:
    str

classes.name: splinter.exceptions.DriverNotFoundError
documentation:
Exception raised when a driver is not found.

Example:

    >>> from splinter import Browser
    >>> b = Browser('unknown driver') # raises DriverNotFoundError

classes.name: splinter.exceptions.ElementDoesNotExist
documentation:
Exception raised when an element is not found in the page.

The exception is raised only when someone tries to access the element,
not when the driver is finding it.

Example:

    >>> elements = browser.find_by_id('unknown-id') # returns an empty list
    >>> elements[0] # raises ElementDoesNotExist

classes.name: splinter.config.Config
documentation:
Standard interface for basic and nearly universal driver flags.

The primary purpose of Config is to reduce the burden on the user to import
Selenium Options objects for common operations. The second purpose is to
avoid argument bloat and drift for the various drivers.

Both Splinter Config and Selenium Options can be used together.
Config will override Options, if applicable.

Config is not a complete replacement for Selenium Options.
For unique and esoteric functionality that is exclusive to one web browser,
Selenium Options should still be used. The purpose of Config is to expose
a universal interface for common functionality, not try to capture all
of it.

Example:

    >>> from splinter import Browser, Config
    >>>
    >>>
    >>> my_config = Config(fullscreen=True)
    >>> my_browser = Browser(config=my_config)


Attributes:
    extensions: Add extensions to the browser.
        The full path to each extension must be included.
        When the browser is closed extensions will be deleted from
        the profile, even if the profile is not a temporary one.

    fullscreen: Launch the browser in fullscreen mode.

    headless: Launch the browser in headless mode.
        Requires Chrome 59+ or Firefox 55+.

    incognito: Launch the browser in incognito mode.

    user_agent: Set a custom user_agent.

modules.name: splinter.cookie_manager
documentation: Copyright 2012 splinter authors. All rights reserved.
Use of this source code is governed by a BSD-style
license that can be found in the LICENSE file.

classes.name: splinter.cookie_manager.CookieManagerAPI

methods.name: splinter.cookie_manager.CookieManagerAPI.add
documentation:
Add a cookie.

Extra arguments will be used to build the cookie.

Arguments:
    cookie (dict): Each key is an identifier for the cookie value.

Examples:

    >>> cookie_manager.add({'name': 'Tony'})
    >>> browser.cookies.add({'cookie_name': 'cookie_value'}, path='/cookiePath')

methods.name: splinter.cookie_manager.CookieManagerAPI.delete
documentation:
Delete one or more cookies.

You can pass all the cookies identifier that you want to delete.

Arguments:
    cookies (list): Identifiers for each cookie to delete.

Examples:

    >>> cookie_manager.delete(
        'name', 'birthday', 'favorite_color') # deletes these three cookies
    >>> cookie_manager.delete('name') # deletes one cookie

methods.name: splinter.cookie_manager.CookieManagerAPI.delete_all
documentation: Delete all cookies.

methods.name: splinter.cookie_manager.CookieManagerAPI.all
documentation:
Get all of the cookies.

    **Note:** If you're using any webdriver and want more info about
    the cookie, set the `verbose` parameter to `True` (in other
    drivers, it won't make any difference). In this case, this method
    will return a list of dicts, each with one cookie's info.

Examples:

    >>> cookie_manager.add({'name': 'Tony'})
    >>> cookie_manager.all()
    [{'name': 'Tony'}]

Returns:
    All the available cookies.

An API that specifies how a splinter driver deals with cookies.

You can add cookies using the {meth}`add <CookieManagerAPI.add>` method,
and remove one or all cookies using
the {meth}`delete <CookieManagerAPI.delete>` method.

A CookieManager acts like a `dict`, so you can access the value of a
cookie through the [] operator, passing the cookie identifier:

> ```pycon
> >>> cookie_manager.add({'name': 'Tony'})
> >>> assert cookie_manager['name'] == 'Tony'
> ```

functions.name: splinter.browser.get_driver
documentation:
Try to instantiate the driver.

Common selenium errors are caught and a retry attempt occurs.
This can mitigate issues running on Remote WebDriver.

functions.name: splinter.browser.Browser
documentation:
Get a new driver instance.

Extra arguments will be sent to the driver instance.

If there is no driver registered with the provided `driver_name`, this
function will raise a {class}`splinter.exceptions.DriverNotFoundError`
exception.

Arguments:

: driver_name (str): Name of the driver to use.
  retry_count (int): Number of times to try and instantiate the driver.

Returns:

: Driver instance

modules.name: splinter.driver
documentation: This module contains the basic API for splinter drivers and elements.
Copyright 2012 splinter authors. All rights reserved.
Use of this source code is governed by a BSD-style
license that can be found in the LICENSE file.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.title
documentation: Title of current page.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.__enter__
documentation: Context manager to use the browser safely.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.__exit__
documentation: Context manager to use the browser safely.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.html
documentation: Source of current page.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.url
documentation: URL of current page.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.visit
documentation:
Use the browser to navigate to the given URL.

Arguments:
    url (str): URL path.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.new_tab
documentation:
The browser will navigate to the given URL in a new tab.

Arguments:
    url (str): URL path.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.back
documentation:
The browser will navigate to the previous URL in the history.

If there is no previous URL, this method does nothing.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.forward
documentation:
The browser will navigate to the next URL in the history.

If there is no URL to forward, this method does nothing.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.reload
documentation: Revisits the current URL.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.get_alert
documentation:
Change the context for working with alerts and prompts.

For more details, check the {doc}`docs about iframes, alerts and prompts </iframes-and-alerts>`

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.get_iframe
documentation:
Change the context for working with iframes.

For more details, check the {doc}`docs about iframes, alerts and prompts </iframes-and-alerts>`

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.execute_script
documentation:
Execute a piece of JavaScript in the browser. Arguments: script (str): The piece of JavaScript to execute. Example: >>> browser.execute_script('document.getElementById("body").innerHTML = "

Hello world!

"')

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.evaluate_script
documentation:
Similar to {meth}`execute_script <DriverAPI.execute_script>` method.

Execute javascript in the browser and return the value of the expression.

Arguments:

: script (str): The piece of JavaScript to execute.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> assert 4 == browser.evaluate_script('2 + 2')
> ```

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_by_css
documentation:
Return an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`,
using a CSS selector to query the current page content.

Arguments:

: css_selector (str): CSS Selector to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_by_xpath
documentation:
Return an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`,
using a xpath selector to query the current page content.

Arguments:

: xpath (str): Xpath to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_by_name
documentation:
Find elements on the current page by their name.

Return an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`.

Arguments:

: name (str): name to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_by_id
documentation:
Find an element on the current page by its id.

Even when only one element is find, this method returns an instance of
{class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`

Arguments:

: id (str): id to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_by_value
documentation:
Find elements on the current page by their value.

Returns an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`

Arguments:

: value (str): value to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_by_text
documentation:
Find elements on the current page by their text.

Returns an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`

Arguments:

: text (str): text to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_by_tag
documentation:
Find all elements of a given tag in current page.

Returns an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`

Arguments:

: tag (str): tag to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_option_by_value
documentation:
Find `<option>` elements by their value.

Returns an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`

Arguments:

: value (str): value to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.find_option_by_text
documentation:
Finds `<option>` elements by their text.

Returns an instance of {class}`ElementList <splinter.element_list.ElementList>`

Arguments:

: text (str): text to use in the search query.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_text_present
documentation:
Check if a piece of text is on the page.

Arguments:

: text (str): text to use in the search query.
  wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search for the text.

Returns:

: bool: True if finds a match for the `text` and False if not.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.type
documentation:
Type a value into an element.

It's useful to test javascript events like keyPress, keyUp, keyDown, etc.

Arguments:
    name (str): name of the element to enter text into.
    value (str): Value to enter into the element.
    slowly (bool): If True, this function returns an iterator which will type one character per iteration.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.fill
documentation:
Fill the field identified by `name` with the content specified by `value`.

Arguments:

: name (str): name of the element to enter text into.
  value (str): Value to enter into the element.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.fill_form
documentation:
Fill the fields identified by `name` with the content specified by `value` in a dict.

Currently, fill_form supports the following fields: text, password, textarea, checkbox,
radio and select.

Checkboxes should be specified as a boolean in the dict.

Arguments:

: field_values (dict): Values for all the fields in the form, in the pattern of {field name: field value}
  form_id (str): Id of the form to fill. Can be used instead of name.
  name (str): Name of the form to fill.
  ignore_missing (bool): Ignore missing keys in the dict.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.choose
documentation:
Choose a value in a radio buttons group.

Arguments:

: name (str): name of the element to enter text into.
  value (str): Value to choose.

Example:

> You have two radio buttons in a page, with the name `gender` and values 'F' and 'M'.
>
> ```pycon
> >>> browser.choose('gender', 'F')
> ```
>
> Then the female gender will be chosen.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.check
documentation:
Check a checkbox by its name.

Arguments:

: name (str): name of the element to check.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> browser.check("agree-with-terms")
> ```

If you call `browser.check` n times, the checkbox keeps checked, it never get unchecked.

To uncheck a checkbox, take a look in the {meth}`uncheck <DriverAPI.uncheck>` method.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.uncheck
documentation:
Uncheck a checkbox by its name.

Arguments:

: name (str): name of the element to uncheck.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> browser.uncheck("send-me-emails")
> ```

If you call `brower.uncheck` n times, the checkbox keeps unchecked, it never get checked.

To check a checkbox, take a look in the {meth}`check <DriverAPI.check>` method.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.select
documentation:
Select an `<option>` element in an `<select>` element using the `name` of the `<select>` and
the `value` of the `<option>`.

Arguments:

: name (str): name of the option element.
  value (str): Value to select.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> browser.select("state", "NY")
> ```

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.quit
documentation: Quit the browser, closing its windows (if it has one).

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_present_by_css
documentation:
Verify if an element is present in the current page.

Arguments:
    css (str): css selector for the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is present and False if is not present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_not_present_by_css
documentation:
Verify if an element is not present in the current page.

Arguments:
    css (str): css selector for the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is not present and False if is present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_present_by_xpath

Returns:

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_not_present_by_xpath

Arguments:
    xpath (str): xpath of the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is not present and False if is present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_present_by_tag
documentation:
Verify if an element is present in the current page.

Arguments:
    tag (str): tag of the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is present and False if is not present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_not_present_by_tag

Arguments:
    tag (str): tag of the element.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is not present and False if is present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_present_by_name
documentation:
Verify if an element is present in the current page.

Arguments:
    name (str): name of the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is present and False if is not present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_not_present_by_name
documentation:
Verify if an element is not present in the current page.

Arguments:
    name (str): name of the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is not present and False if is present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_present_by_value
documentation:
Verify if an element is present in the current page.

Arguments:
    value (str): value in the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is present and False if is not present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_not_present_by_value
documentation:
Verify if an element is not present in the current page.

Arguments:
    value (str): value in the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is not present and False if is present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_present_by_text
documentation:
Verify if an element is present in the current page.

Arguments:
    text (str): text in the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is present and False if is not present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_not_present_by_text
documentation:
Verify if an element is not present in the current page.

Arguments:
    text (str): text in the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is not present and False if is present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_present_by_id
documentation:
Verify if an element is present in the current page.

Arguments:
    id (str): id for the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is present and False if is not present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.is_element_not_present_by_id
documentation:
Verify if an element is not present in the current page.

Arguments:
    id (str): id for the element.
    wait_time (int): Number of seconds to search.

Returns:
    bool: True if the element is not present and False if is present.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.screenshot
documentation:
Take a screenshot of the current page and save it locally.

Arguments:
    name (str): File name for the screenshot.
    suffix (str): File extension for the screenshot.
    full (bool): If the screenshot should be full screen or not.
    unique_file (bool): If true, the filename will include a path to
        the system temp directory and extra characters at the end to
        ensure the file is unique.

Returns:
    str: Full file name of the created screenshot.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.html_snapshot
documentation:
Write the current html to a file.

Arguments:
    name (str): File name.
    suffix (str): File extension.
    encoding (str): File encoding.
    unique_file (str): If true, the filename will include a path to
        the system temp directory and extra characters at the end to
        ensure the file is unique.

Returns:
    str: Full file name of the created html snapshot.

methods.name: splinter.driver.DriverAPI.cookies
documentation:
A {class}`CookieManager <splinter.cookie_manager.CookieManagerAPI>` instance.

For more details, check the {doc}`cookies manipulation section </cookies>`.
Basic driver API class.

classes.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.shadow_root
documentation: Get the shadow root of an element's shadow DOM.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.text
documentation: All of the text within the element.  HTML tags are stripped.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.click
documentation: Click the element.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.check
documentation:
Check the element, if it's "checkable" (e.g.: a checkbox).

If the element is already checked, this method does nothing. For unchecking
elements, take a loot in the {meth}`uncheck <ElementAPI.uncheck>` method.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.uncheck
documentation:
Uncheck the element, if it's "checkable" (e.g.: a checkbox).

If the element is already unchecked, this method does nothing. For checking
elements, take a loot in the {meth}`check <ElementAPI.check>` method.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.checked
documentation:
Get the checked status of the element.

Example:

    >>> element.check()
    >>> assert element.checked
    >>> element.uncheck()
    >>> assert not element.checked

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.visible
documentation:
Get the current visibility status of the element.

Returns:
    bool

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.is_visible
documentation:
Check if an element is visible within the given wait time.

Arguments:
    wait_time (int): Time in seconds to check for the element.

Returns:
    bool

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.is_not_visible
documentation:
Check if an element is not visible within the given wait time.

Arguments:
    wait_time (int): Time in seconds to check for the element.

Returns:
    bool

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.has_class
documentation: Indicates whether the element has the given class.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.mouse_over
documentation: Move the mouse over the element.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.mouse_out
documentation: Move the mouse away from the element.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.clear
documentation: Reset the field value.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.fill
documentation:
Fill the element with text content.

Arguments:
    value (str): Value to enter into the element.

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.type
documentation:
Type the `value` in the element.

If `slowly` is True, this function returns an iterator which will type one character per iteration.

It's useful to test javascript events like keyPress, keyUp, keyDown, etc.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> from selenium.webdriver.common.keys import Keys
> >>> ElementAPI.type(Keys.RETURN)
> ```

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.select
documentation:
Select an `<option>` element in the element using the `value` of the `<option>`.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> element.select("NY")
> ```

methods.name: splinter.driver.ElementAPI.screenshot
documentation: Take a screenshot of the element.

Basic element API class.

Any element in the page can be represented as an instance of `ElementAPI`.

Once you have an instance, you can easily access attributes like a `dict`:

> ```pycon
> >>> element = browser.find_by_id("link-logo").first
> >>> assert element['href'] == 'https://splinter.readthedocs.io'
> ```

You can also interact with the instance using the methods and properties listed below.
